title: South Korea Launches ROKS Jeju Frigate with Advanced Air Defense and Strike Capabilities
summary: South Korea's navy launched the ROKS Jeju frigate equipped with phased-array radar and K-VLS vertical launch system. The vessel enhances regional missile defense and naval strike capabilities.[2]
contents:
# South Korea Launches ROKS Jeju Frigate with Advanced Air Defense and Strike Capabilities

## Overview
South Korea's navy launched the ROKS Jeju frigate equipped with phased-array radar and K-VLS vertical launch system. The vessel enhances regional missile defense and naval strike capabilities.[2]

## Details
The ROKS Jeju remains a new frigate designed to boost South Korea's naval power amid rising regional tensions. It features advanced phased-array radar for superior detection and the K-VLS system for launching missiles.[2] This launch aligns with South Korea's ongoing defense modernization efforts, including exports like arms to Europe valued at roughly $11 billion in 2025.[3] No specific names of officials or exact launch ceremony details stayed reported in the coverage. The frigate's capabilities focus on air defense and strike, strengthening deterrence against potential threats.[2]

## Location
South Korea

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @KAngDaily (Korea Ang Daily). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.
